key_insight: The paper replaces nonconvex PINN training with a convex, per-iteration least-squares solve by combining Bellman-Kalaba quasilinearization with linear-in-parameters trial spaces.
engineering_relevance: This offers a more stable and often faster way to solve nonlinear PDEs in engineering, especially when accurate physics-informed solutions are needed without gradient-based optimization.
method: Quasilinearize the nonlinear PDE into a sequence of linear subproblems.
Discretize each subproblem by collocation on LiL trial spaces such as random features, spectral polynomials, or trigonometric bases.
Solve each step with a direct QR-based linear least-squares method instead of backpropagation.
evidence_signal: The method was tested on seven benchmark PDE problems and typically converged in single-digit outer iterations, matching or surpassing published PINNs on Navier-Stokes while using far fewer trainable parameters.
watch_item: The convergence guarantee is local and depends on a smallness condition, so performance may degrade if the initial guess or nonlinearity is too strong.
paper_title: A Convex Quasilinearization Method for Solving Nonlinear PDEs with Physics-Informed Neural Networks
